blocks.text: A. To encourage the practice and competition of golf among handicap players within the Federation.
B. To hold various activities throughout the year that enable training for young people and adults
on different golf-related topics, such as talks, clinics, camps, etc.
C. To achieve an ordering of players with a view to forming teams (Inter-Federation),
participation in national tournaments and other training programmes that require selecting players from this Federation.
D. To promote the true values of golf: integrity and honesty, consideration towards
other players and respect for the rules and regulations of the game.
A. Exclusively, all Under 23, Junior and Pre-Junior players with AAG membership and
handicap index who are members of and/or represent entities affiliated with the Federation may participate, provided they have their permanent place of residence within said region.
1. JUNIORS (M18) – Women and Men: For 2026: players born in 2008,
2. PRE-JUNIORS (M15) – Women and Men: For 2026: born between 2011 and
2016, both years inclusive.
3. UNDER 23: players who had not reached the age of 22 by 31 December of the year prior to
the year in which the Championship is held.
B. Likewise, each player registered in the Junior (M18) or Pre-Junior (M15) category must
comply with the educational obligations prescribed under applicable legislation. The FGL Championships Committee may request documentation proving such status at any time.
C. Players who do not belong to the Federation and are invited by the

Organisation may participate. In such cases, they will take part in the Tournament but not in the Ranking, which will be exclusive to Federation players. Positions, solely for scoring purposes, will be those resulting from excluding non-Federation players from the final tournament standings.
D. ALBATROS JUNIORS – OPTION TO PARTICIPATE IN THE FGL PRE-JUNIOR (M15) RANKING

(M15): ALBATROS category players (FGL Junior Ranking/FGL) who hold AAG membership and a current handicap index may opt to also compete for the FGL Pre-Junior (M15) Ranking on the dates they choose, subject to the following conditions:
1. Prior option: the player must declare this option at the time of registration.
2. Format and tees: if opting for the Pre-Junior Ranking, they must play the same format and
conditions as the Pre-Junior category (course/tees/36 or 18 holes as applicable), with GROSS scoring.
3. Scoring: on those dates, the player will earn points in the Pre-Junior (M15) Ranking

according to their position in the Pre-Junior (M15) standings, considering exclusively those competing for said ranking (if they also participate as a "Junior", this does not alter the ranking score).
4. Compatibility with AAG regulations: eligibility for AAG spots or competitions will
be governed by the current AAG regulations (age, conditions, etc.).
5. FGL authority: the FGL Championships Committee may accept or reject this
option on a given date for reasons of capacity, logistics or sporting criteria, and must state this in the invitation.
A. The Federation will determine the dates and venues of each competition. Competition conditions will
be those set by AAG rules for competitions organised and sponsored by it.
B. A GENERAL RANKING of Under 23, Junior and Pre-Junior players combined will be maintained for the pre-
selection of combined teams from different categories and for selected spots in AAG High Performance tournaments and training.
C. All tournaments and the annual Ranking for the Under 23, Junior and Pre-Junior categories will
be played in GROSS format (without handicap allowance), with positions determined exclusively by the gross score obtained by each player.
D. Notwithstanding the above, each tournament will award a special prize for the best overall
NETO score, taking into account the player's current handicap index in accordance with AAG regulations. This prize will not affect gross standings or the Federation Ranking.
A. In handicap categories, play will be conducted with the handicap index corresponding to the date of
each tournament, in accordance with AAG official statistics and publications.
B. Recent handicap registrations: Players who have recently obtained a handicap

(who are not yet listed on the latest register) may also participate. To do so, these players must provide authorisation from the club they represent, stating registration date, membership number and current handicap.
A. Players may not receive assistance or carry caddies, but may use manual
trolleys to carry their equipment. The use of battery-powered manual trolleys, self-propelled trolleys or golf carts is not permitted.

title: VI. TEAM FORMATION/FEDERATION SPOTS IN NATIONAL JUNIOR

blocks.text: AND PRE-JUNIOR CHAMPIONSHIPS:
A. The formation of combined teams across different classes and/or categories (for example: Inter-
Federation National Tournament) will be carried out using the GENERAL RANKING.
B. The selection of players who will occupy Federation spots for AAG Junior and Pre-Junior
national tournaments will be determined according to AAG guidelines (see
Annex I)
C. The Federation reserves the right, if necessary, to select directly

one or more players. The player designated at the Federation's discretion will be selected by it, taking into account their record, score, Federation ranking, national rankings, club ranking, amateur ranking, university golf, participation in Federation tournaments, conduct and any other criteria deemed necessary for such purposes.
A. The winner of the Ranking in each category will be the player who obtains the highest number of
points in the corresponding category after applying the drops provided for in the regulations.
B. In the event of a tie for first place, the Ranking winner will be the player who has won Ranking Tournaments the greatest
number of times. If no decision is reached, the same criterion will be applied considering second places obtained, and so on.
C. The preceding criterion will apply to break ties for subsequent places.
D. The annual Ranking of the Federación de Golf del Litoral will be compiled exclusively on the basis of
gross results.
A. The Calendar will be announced together with the dates of the remaining FGL tournaments.

With the aim of enabling its Under 23, Junior and Pre-Junior players to participate in as many tournaments as possible, the Federation requires participation in a minimum of 3 of the 4 tournaments to be held (if participating in more than 3 tournaments, the 3 best scores will be counted, discarding the rest). All dates, in order to enable points for AAG ranking spots, will be over 36 holes.
B. Score Calculation:
1. Points distribution in each competition will be assigned as follows: 1st 20
points, 2nd 16 points, 3rd 12 points, 4th 8 points, 5th 4 points.
2. Only if all 4 competitions have been completed will a
drop of the lowest score among the tournaments played by each player be implemented.
3. For tournaments reduced to 18 holes due to force majeure, 50% of the
score obtained will be counted.
4. Bonus points will be awarded according to the gross score of each 18-hole round relative to

the course rating of the field played. Said bonus will be one point per stroke for those players whose score is equal to or below: a. the course rating, for Under 23, Women and Men b. the course rating plus 2 strokes, for Juniors (M18) – Women and Men c. the course rating plus 4 strokes, for Pre-Juniors (M15) – Women and Men.
5. This is calculated by rounding to the nearest negative integer the difference between the score and
the rating. Maximum bonus score per round: 5 points.
6. The FGL championships committee is authorised to round said rating up or down according to
the actual difficulty of presentation and/or distances implemented.
7. Bonus scores are independent of the score for each tournament, and therefore are NOT
dropped
C. Scheduled hole reductions:
1. If a competition scheduled for 36 holes, by decision of the Tournament Committee and

whatever the reasons for the reduction, is played over 18 holes, 50% of the points (with decimal rounding) of the score detailed above will be awarded. For the purposes of these Regulations, it will count as an 18-hole tournament.
2. If a 36-hole tournament had to conclude with 27(+) holes played, the
full score will be awarded.
3. A tournament scheduled for 36 holes that is reduced due to force majeure to fewer than 18
holes will count as valid, and the organising federation may hold another in its place.
D. Ties:
1. In the event of a tie in a position, the points to be awarded for that place will be calculated

by adding the points assigned for each of the positions involved, divided by the number of players in those places. Figures with decimals will be rounded to one decimal place. NOTE: Justified absences will include participation in international tournaments and in all AAG tournaments that award points for the annual ranking, as well as absence due to illness or force majeure. Under 23, Junior and Pre-Junior players who participate in FGL Tournaments must, in all cases except force majeure, submit their scorecard upon completion. Failure to comply with the above may result in a sanction for the player ranging from point deduction to suspension for one event, and in the event of repeat offences they will not be considered for the Inter-Federation team or other selection events.
A. No coach or instructor should approach players during the course of a

round. Family members, companions or spectators must refrain from repeatedly approaching competitors during a round. In both cases, failure to observe the above will constitute a strong presumption of breach of Rule 10.2 on "Advice".
A. The FGL Championships Committee will be responsible for administering the Ranking,

setting the number of tournaments to be held annually, the designation of venues in conjunction with each Club, the dates of each competition, handicap index limits, Ranking control, review of the score assigned in each competition, etc., as well as any other unforeseen matter.
B. Each competition will be played in accordance with the conditions set out in these
C. If due to adverse weather conditions (frost, rain, fog, etc.) it is necessary to postpone
tee times or temporarily suspend play, and this makes it difficult to complete a round, the Tournament Committee is authorised to reduce the number of holes to be played, in some or all categories.
D. Tee times for the second 18 holes will be arranged by score order in each

category. Nevertheless, the Committee reserves the right to modify that order in the case of individual requests of exceptional urgency, provided the players involved are not placed in the leading positions.
E. First place will be decided automatically according to the following criterion: a)
best last 18 holes; b) best last 9 holes (10 to 18); c) best last 6, 3, 2 or 1 holes until the tie is broken; or d) draw.
F. The Tournament Committee reserves the right to issue new Conditions (or Local Rules) or
amend any of those in force for reasons of necessity and opportunity, which will be communicated to competitors through information posted on the notice board and/or delivered in person.
G. Local Rules in force for each competition will be communicated prior to the start.
A. To take charge of the general organisation of the Tournament, regardless of the work carried out by
the Federation and/or personnel designated by it.
B. To present the course in the best possible conditions, informing the Federation
of any serious situation that could be grounds for suspending the Tournament with sufficient advance notice.
C. The host club must welcome PLAYERS with breakfast.
D. The host club must provide a practice area so that players can warm up with their coaches, if
required, and DRIVING RANGE OR IMPROVISED PRACTICE AREA service, PREVIOUSLY ANNOUNCED TO THE FEDERATION.
E. The host club will provide (at its expense) lunch for the players, which may be served as
they finish playing, or at a time determined by the club. It must also offer 2 additional menus for players' family members.
F. The host club will be responsible for announcing tee times (starter), receiving score
cards and processing and publishing/announcing results. It will also be responsible for presenting the corresponding prizes.
A. On FGL ranking dates, the registration fee for Under 23, Junior and
Pre-Junior players will be covered by the Federación de Golf del Litoral, with the amount for 18 and 36 holes respectively set by the Committee.
B. Registrations will be accepted until 6:00 p.m., two days before the tournament,

unless another date is specified in the relevant publication, and will be accepted ONLY through the official FGL registration platform or, failing that, through the channels indicated by the Federation in the tournament invitation.
C. Telephone registrations or those without the corresponding application will not be accepted. Incomplete
applications will also not be admitted.
D. The organising club will be responsible for arranging tee times using the registration
sheet provided by the FGL
E. The player must be registered in order to register, except in exceptional
cases (e.g.: Invited Players, etc.)
F. Registration alone implies knowledge of and compliance with the provisions of these Regulations.
G. Properly completed registrations submitted after the registration deadline

will be subject to availability of spots in each category. Note: On AAG Junior and Pre-Junior National Championship dates in all their categories, the Federation will pay at least 50% of the registration fees for all players who participate.

AAG Spot Allocation for Junior and Pre-Junior National Tournaments - 2026 Season In accordance with the provisions of the Asociación Argentina de Golf (AAG) regarding the system of spot allocation per Regional Federation for the 2026 Junior and Pre-Junior National Tournaments, the Federación de Golf del Litoral (FGL) establishes the following internal qualification mechanism for the nomination of players.
1. Regulatory Framework

The AAG allocates Federation spots on an unnamed basis, i.e. awarded to the Federation and not to specific players, in accordance with the Argentine Ranking 2025 standings and subsequent recalculations during the 2026 season. Regional Federations must establish a mixed qualification system based on:

items: Results from regional tournaments or selection events
Sporting merit where applicable (respecting handicap limits established by AAG)

blocks.text: 2. FGL General Qualification Criteria

For the nomination of spots allocated to the FGL in each National Tournament, the following order of priority will apply: 2.1 Automatic Qualification by FGL Ranking The following will receive automatic priority:

items: The player highest placed in the current FGL Ranking in each category.
Players placed within the order required to fill the spots allocated to the FGL by the AAG.

blocks.text: The FGL Ranking will be updated after each countable competition and published officially.
3. Categories Considered
Qualification will respect the categories established by AAG:
4. Tie-Breaking System in the FGL Ranking
In the event of equal points in the FGL Ranking at the time of registration closing, the following tie-breaking order will apply:
1. Best position in the last Federation tournament played.
2. If equality persists, resolution by the FGL Competitions Committee.
5. Spots by Sporting Merit

If the FGL Executive Committee deems it appropriate to designate a player by sporting merit, they must mandatorily comply with the handicap index limits established by the AAG for each category. Such designation:

items: May not exceed the total spot allocation assigned to the FGL.
Will be formally communicated to the AAG.

blocks.text: 6. Spots Secured by National Ranking

If an FGL player obtains a secured spot by placement in the National Ranking (as established by the AAG after each National Tournament), said spot will be considered included within the allocated Federation spot and will not generate an additional place
7. Spot Transfers between Categories

The FGL may, at its sole discretion, by applying a maximum average score requirement during Federation tournaments, request the transfer of up to two (2) spots between categories, as permitted by AAG regulations, formalising the request in a timely manner before the AAG Championships Committee. For this purpose, the following maximum reference average scores are established for each category, which will be used to guarantee spot allocation:

If a player exceeds the maximum average score established for their category, the Federation may reassign said spot to another player of the same or a different category who demonstrates better sporting performance, respecting current regulatory criteria. The Federation reserves the right to modify these reference values during the course of the season, based on the evolution of competitive level, playing conditions, recorded results and guidelines established by the AAG. Any modification will be duly communicated officially and applied prospectively.
8. Registration Formalisation

items: The FGL will carry out the official nomination through the online spot allocation form.
Each player must also complete the individual registration form required by the AAG.
Registration will be confirmed only when both steps have been completed.

blocks.text: 9. Committee Powers
The FGL Competitions Committee may resolve situations not foreseen in this Annex, always respecting the AAG regulatory framework.
